# Transcrição: Does_money_lead_to_happiness_

- **Fonte:** Does_money_lead_to_happiness_
- **Duração:** 01:00
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:00]

What I thought was the most interesting part of this book was when they did the research study on was there a correlation between people becoming happier as they made more money and they found that there was no correlation between more money and happiness. However, there was a correlation between happiness and control of your time. When you wake up in the morning and you can do what you want, when you want, with who you want, wherever you want, for as long as you want, that is a better dictator of you being happy as compared to you making more money. It's freedom and its control of time. That is such a fascinating view because for the longest time all those around me when I was young, happiness seems to have come from possessions. When uncle got the bigger house or the whole family came over and everyone seemed so happy and he was so happy and I thought, oh that's happiness. You have a boat, oh get a boat, he seems super happy in his boat. So that's why I've always had that mindset of money does bring you happiness because it brings you more material things and possessions. But this was one of the first few times I read it and went huh it's not about that, it's about you being able to do what you want to do.
